body: @sepo-agent /answer check what others similar fixes need for this?

comments.body:
I checked issue #28 and there is no parent/tracking issue linked. The closest related work is open PR #21, which already identifies the same install/update gap plus a few adjacent runner-stability fixes: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/21

What I’d include for this issue:

1. Add/keep generated-output exclusions in both install/update skills:
   - [`install-agent`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.skills/install-agent/SKILL.md#L56) and [`update-agent`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.skills/update-agent/SKILL.md#L59) already say not to copy `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `.agent/node_modules/`, `.agent/dist/`, etc.
   - They should also explicitly say to **merge** `.agent/dist/` and `.agent/node_modules/` into the target repo’s `.gitignore`.

2. Update install docs:
   - [`install-existing-repository.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/deployment/install-existing-repository.md#L11) currently says to copy `.agent/` and `.github/`, but does not mention `.gitignore`.
   - Add a short generated-output rule block there.

3. Update this repo’s own `.gitignore`:
   - Current file has `.agent/dist/` but not `.agent/node_modules/`: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.gitignore#L1-L4
   - Add `.agent/node_modules/`.

4. Regression/test coverage:
   - Since this is docs/skill guidance, a lightweight envelope/fixture assertion is enough if we want to pin the contract.
   - Similar fixes in PR #21 used `.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts` to assert workflow/doc invariants.

Important caveat from PR #21: I would **not** bundle this with the `checkout-pr` cleanup change unless we also fix the runtime risk called out in review. Deleting `.agent/dist` before checkout can break later `node .agent/dist/...` steps if the PR branch does not track dist. For issue #28, the safer scope is just: skill guidance + install docs + `.gitignore` + focused assertion.

title: Add including `dist (and node modules etc) ` for the installation skills
